title: Implement azq form build as a thin command-layer writer
goal: Make azq/formam/build.py coordinate exact parent-goal validation, deterministic stub deliverable creation, and canonical Formam writes without turning Formam into a planning or task engine.
context: Stage 2 defines Formam as the stage that turns goals into deliverables and dependency maps.
Wave A already established canonical Formam storage, normalization, parsing, exact lookup, parent-goal validation, and write helpers.
The implementation plan says azq form build <goal_id> should generate a stub deliverable and a goal map rather than trying to solve full planning on day one.
Formam must define boundaries of work, not task lists, scheduling, or Agenda behavior.
requirements: Create azq/formam/build.py.
Add a thin command-layer entrypoint such as build_form(goal_id).
Validate the parent goal by exact goal_id through the canonical Formam/Finis validation helper.
Allocate the next stable DELIV_### id through azq/formam/storage.py.
Generate one deterministic stub deliverable record from the validated goal data.
Write one canonical deliverable file through azq/formam/storage.py.
Write or refresh one canonical goal map file through azq/formam/storage.py.
Keep the behavior minimal, inspectable, and free of Agenda-style task generation or speculative planning logic.
acceptance_criteria: A valid canonical goal can produce one DELIV_###.md file and one GOAL_<goal_id>_MAP.md file.
build.py delegates persistence and file-format decisions to azq/formam/storage.py rather than writing files ad hoc.
The first build behavior remains intentionally small, deterministic, and human-readable.
suggested_commit_message: add form build command module for canonical deliverables

title: Implement azq form list as a thin presentation layer
goal: Make azq/formam/deliverables.py provide a read-only listing view over canonical Formam storage so deliverables become visible from the command layer.
context: The Stage 2 implementation plan calls for azq form list as one of the first public Formam commands.
Wave B should expose read commands only after the first canonical write path exists.
The list command should sit on top of canonical Formam storage rather than duplicating repository logic or introducing search features.
requirements: Create azq/formam/deliverables.py.
Add a read-only listing function such as list_deliverables().
Load canonical deliverables through azq/formam/storage.py.
Display at least deliverable_id, goal_id, title, and status.
Keep the output simple, stable, and terminal-readable.
Preserve a clear no-deliverables message when canonical storage is empty.
acceptance_criteria: deliverables.py can present canonical deliverables without mutating state.
The listing reads through storage.py rather than duplicating repository logic.
The visible output stays focused on deliverables, not tasks, scheduling, or execution.

title: Implement azq form show with exact-id deliverable inspection
goal: Extend azq/formam/deliverables.py so one canonical deliverable can be inspected clearly by exact deliverable_id.
context: The Stage 2 implementation plan calls for azq form show <deliverable_id> as a first-class Formam read command.
Exact-id lookup matters because later Agenda work will depend on stable deliverable identifiers.
This command should inspect one existing deliverable and stop there rather than broadening into filtering, searching, or planning features.
requirements: Add a reader such as show_deliverable(deliverable_id).
Load one deliverable by exact deliverable_id through azq/formam/storage.py.
Print the canonical deliverable fields clearly, including artifact_description and dependencies.
Handle missing deliverable ids with a readable exact-id failure message.
Keep output centered on artifact definition and parent linkage rather than task speculation.
acceptance_criteria: One canonical deliverable can be inspected by exact id.
Missing deliverables fail clearly without fuzzy matching.
deliverables.py remains a presentation module over shared storage helpers.
suggested_commit_message: add exact deliverable inspection for form show

title: Implement azq form map as a thin command-layer map refresher
goal: Make azq/formam/maps.py generate or refresh a canonical goal map from the current deliverables for one exact goal.
context: The Stage 2 implementation plan calls for azq form map <goal_id> to create or refresh the visible dependency structure for a goal.
Wave A already defined canonical map storage, normalization, parsing, and write helpers.
Map output should remain human-readable and conservative even when dependency data is sparse.
Formam maps should record deliverable relationships, not hidden planning state.
requirements: Create azq/formam/maps.py.
Add a command-layer entrypoint such as build_goal_map(goal_id) or refresh_goal_map(goal_id).
Validate the parent goal by exact goal_id through the canonical validation helper.
Load deliverables for that exact goal through azq/formam/storage.py.
Write or refresh one canonical GOAL_<goal_id>_MAP.md file through azq/formam/storage.py.
If dependency edges are derived automatically, derive them conservatively from existing deliverable dependency lists rather than inventing planning structure.
Do not introduce task generation, scheduling, or Agenda behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A goal with deliverables can produce or refresh one visible canonical map artifact.
Map regeneration depends on canonical deliverables for the exact parent goal.
maps.py remains a thin command-layer module over shared storage helpers.
suggested_commit_message: add form goal map command module

title: Wire Formam commands into the CLI
goal: Expose the first usable Formam loop in azq/cli.py without broadening beyond the Stage 2 command set.
context: The live CLI currently exposes Scintilla and Finis command families, but not Formam.
The Stage 2 implementation plan defines the first Formam command set as azq form build, azq form list, azq form show, and azq form map.
Canonical goals already depend on ensure_finis_storage_ready(), so Formam command routing should keep goal-backed operations aligned with the Stage 1 migration boundary.
Wave B is complete when the repository has a first usable Formam command loop.
requirements: Add command help lines for azq form build <goal_id>, azq form list, azq form show <deliverable_id>, and azq form map <goal_id>.
Add a form branch in azq/cli.py with clear usage messages for each supported subcommand.
Route build, list, show, and map to the new azq/formam modules.
Run ensure_finis_storage_ready() before Formam commands that depend on canonical goals.
Keep command handling narrow and stylistically consistent with the existing CLI.
Do not introduce Agenda, doctor, status, or task behavior in this task.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI exposes the first usable Formam command loop.
azq form build, azq form list, azq form show, and azq form map dispatch to dedicated Formam modules.
CLI help and usage text stay aligned with the current Stage 2 scope.
